title: Income Tax & Financial Planning Practice Set
description: Comprehensive practice questions to test your knowledge of individual tax exemptions, capital gains, deductions, and tax compliance.

questions.question: What is the standard deduction limit available to salaried individuals for the financial year under the default tax regime?
questions.explanation: Salaried individuals are entitled to a flat standard deduction of 50,000 from their gross salary income under both old and new regimes.

questions.question: Which section of the Income Tax Act allows exemption on long-term capital gains arising from the sale of a residential house property if reinvested in another residential house?
questions.explanation: Section 54 provides tax exemption on long-term capital gains from selling a residential property, provided the gains are reinvested in buying or constructing another residential house within the specified time.

questions.question: What is the maximum annual contribution limit in a Public Provident Fund (PPF) account to claim tax benefits?
questions.explanation: Under Section 80C, the maximum investment limit allowed in a Public Provident Fund (PPF) account is 150,000 per financial year.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the Minimum Alternate Tax (MAT) rate applied to book profits of companies under Section 115JB?
questions.explanation: The basic rate of Minimum Alternate Tax (MAT) under Section 115JB is 15.0% of the book profits of the corporation, plus applicable surcharge and cess.

questions.question: What is the penalty for filing an Income Tax Return (ITR) after the due date, if the total income exceeds 500,000?
questions.explanation: Under Section 234F, the late filing fee is 5,000 if the return is filed after the due date, and restricted to 1,000 if the total income does not exceed 500,000.

questions.question: In which head of income are the earnings of a professional partner from a partnership firm taxed?
options: Profits and Gains of Business or Profession (PGBP)
questions.explanation: Salary, bonus, commission, or interest received by a partner from a firm is taxed under the head 'Profits and Gains of Business or Profession (PGBP)' rather than 'Salaries'.

questions.question: What is the maximum deduction allowed for interest paid on a home loan for a self-occupied property under Section 24(b)?
questions.explanation: Under Section 24(b), the deduction for interest on housing loan for a self-occupied property is capped at a maximum of 200,000 per financial year.

questions.question: TDS on payments to contractors is deducted under which section of the Income Tax Act?
questions.explanation: Section 194C of the Income Tax Act governs Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) for payments made to resident contractors or sub-contractors.
